title: Post-graduation work permit: How to find your language level based on your test results - Canada.ca
content:
Post-graduation work permit

## How to find your language level based on your test results
If you apply on or after November 1, 2024, you must provide proof of English or French language skills. The only exception is if you graduate from a PGWP-eligible flight school.
You don’t need to provide proof of language ability if you
- submitted your PGWP application before November 1, 2024, regardless of your level of study
- graduated from a PGWP-eligible flight school
To measure your English or French levels, we use
- Canadian Language Benchmarks (CLB) for English
- Niveaux de compétence linguistique canadiens (NCLC) (in French only) for French
Use your test results to find your CLB or NCLC level in the tables below:
- IELTS (General Training)

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
